title: Vanden Huyck Willard
content:
{{Top|legends=Vanden Willard/Legends}} {{Update|[[Star Wars: Geektionary: The Galaxy from A - Z]], [[The Rebel Alliance (reference book)|The Rebel Alliance]], [[Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide]], [[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Vanden_Willard2.jpg]] |name=Vanden Huyck Willard |homeworld=*[[Alderaan]]{{EncyclopediaCite|The Rebel Alliance}} *[[Sulorine]]''[[Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide]]'' |birth= |death=[[1 ABY]],According to ''[[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition]]'', the [[Battle of Mako-Ta]] occurred around a [[Standard year|year]] after the [[Battle of Yavin]]. As ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' establishes that the battle marks the beginning of [[0 ABY]], the Assault, including Willard's [[death]], must take place around [[1 ABY]]. ''[[Yavin's Hope]]'', near the [[Mako-Ta Space Docks]][[Star Wars (2015) 50|''Star Wars'' (2015) 50]] |species=[[Human]]{{Film|IV}} {{C|[[Alderaanian]]}} |gender=[[Gender|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=1.90 [[meter]]s |mass= |hair=[[Color|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Massassi Group]] **[[Alliance High Command]][[Star Wars (2015) 45|''Star Wars'' (2015) 45]] **[[Delta Group]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You're safe. When we heard about Alderaan, we feared the worst.|Vanden Willard, greeting Leia Organa on Yavin 4|}} '''Vanden Huyck Willard''' was a [[human]] [[Gender|male]] who served as a [[sector commander]] and [[general]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]]. He served at the [[Great Temple|Rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4]] during the [[Battle of Yavin]]. He was [[Death|killed]] when his cruiser ''[[Yavin's Hope]]'' was attacked by the ''[[Executor]]'' near the [[Mako-Ta Space Docks]]. ==Biography== ===On Yavin 4=== {{Quote|Where's Commander Willard?''"
"''Waiting for you inside the Great Temple.|Organa and a rebel, upon her arrival on Yavin 4|''[[A New Hope: The Princess, the Scoundrel, and the Farm Boy]]''}} [[File:Willard_Leia_ANH.jpg|thumb|left|250px|General Willard reunions with Leia Organa on Yavin 4.]] Vanden Huyck Willard was a [[sector commander]] and [[general]]''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]'' in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] at the [[time]] of the [[Battle of Yavin]]. After [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] was [[Rescue of Princess Leia|rescued]] from aboard the [[Galactic Empire]]'s [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] by [[Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca]], [[C-3PO]], and [[R2-D2]], and returned to the [[Great Temple|hidden rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4]], Willard greeted them and was glad to know Organa was safe, as when they heard about the [[Destruction of Alderaan|destruction]] of [[Alderaan]], they feared the worst. When it was discovered that the [[Starship|ship]] they had used, the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'', was tracked and that the [[Death Star]] had arrived. Willard was responsible for reviewing the [[flight simulator]] records of any [[pilot]] who wished to fly an [[X-wing starfighter]] for the Rebel Alliance prior to granting their recruitment. Willard later helped coordinate the battle against the [[superweapon]]. During the battle, Willard was in the war room with a [[Unidentified control officer|control officer]], General [[Jan Dodonna]], [[Colonel]] [[Bandwin Cor]], and Organa. When Dodonna asked whether there were any other reversion signatures, Willard replied that it was just the Death Star's. A partial transcript of recordings from the war room, which included Willard's comment, was eventually placed in ''[[The Rebel Files]]''. ===At Mako-Ta Rebel Base=== {{Quote|No. That's……Willard's ship.|Leia Organa, on witnessing the attack on Willard's cruiser|}} [[File:DeltaGroup-2015StarWars50.png|thumb|right|250px|General Willard in his cruiser when the rebels were under attack.]] After a failed meeting with [[Regent]] [[Dors Urtya]] on [[Mon Cala]] in an attempt to recruit the [[Mon Cala Mercantile Fleet]], Willard was present at the [[Mako-Ta Space Docks]] in a meeting of [[Alliance High Command]] when [[Chancellor (Rebel Alliance)|Chancellor]] [[Mon Mothma]] requested the report on Mon Cala by Organa. As Organa stated that Urtya would never lead a mutiny against the Empire, Willard reacted with disappointment. Organa continued, and outlined her plan to rescue [[King of Mon Cala|King]] [[Lee-Char]], in order to secure an alliance with the [[Mon Calamari]] and incorporate the [[fleet]] into their own [[Rebel Alliance Navy|naval forces]]. Eventually, Organa and [[Heroes of Yavin|her team]] were able to breach a [[Unidentified Imperial base (Strokill Prime)|prison]] on [[Strokill Prime]] and find Lee-Char. Too injured to move, they recorded his final words before he was [[Death|killed]] by Imperial [[stormtrooper]]s.[[Star Wars (2015) 48|''Star Wars'' (2015) 48]] Although Urtya was able to recover the recordings from Organa, he sent the message to the [[Fleet Captain]]s of the Mon Cala fleet, resulting in their [[Mutiny on Mon Cala|mutiny]] and defection to the Rebel Alliance.[[Star Wars (2015) 49|''Star Wars'' (2015) 49]] After the mutiny, Willard was given command of ''[[Yavin's Hope]]'' that had been refitted for combat by the [[Kingdom of Shu-Torun]], by order of [[Queen]] [[Trios]]. Willard utilized the [[cruiser]] as the [[flagship]] of [[Delta Group]] and remained present at the Mako-Ta Space Docks during a ceremony celebrating the new Alliance Fleet and for rebel personnel and rebel allies to witness the dispersal of the rebel [[Navy|naval]] forces. During the ceremony, Willard invited Organa to his cruiser, but she turned down the request, preferring to be with Mothma just in case she was needed. Prior to launch, Organa asked for a check-in of sub-groups, and Willard was the final one to confirm that group had no issues. Willard also told Organa to tell Mothma good luck for her speech, believing that she would stun the crowd. After Organa informed Mothma that the fleet was ready, she also mentioned Willard's statement, and told Mothma that he knew he would be impressed. As the ships were ready to launch into [[hyperspace]], an error prevented the launch of the Willard's cruiser, as well as all the other ships that had been refitted with [[Shu-Torun]] technology. The fault resulted in the failure of all launches, lost communications, disabled armament, and the ships being unable to launch their [[starfighter]]s. At the same [[time]], [[Death Squadron]], under the command of the [[Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]], [[Battle of Mako-Ta|arrived]] and fired on the nearest cruiser, Willard's starship. ===Death=== {{Quote|Quickly, abandon ship! May the Force be with us al—|Vanden Willard's final words as his ship is destroyed|}} [[File:WillardsDeath-2015StarWars50.png|thumb|left|225px|Willard's final moments aboard his cruiser]] As Willard's cruiser was attacked by the ''[[Executor]]'' and an accompanying [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], Mothma, Organa, General [[Davits Draven]], and other rebel personnel watched on in horror from the base. Consistently pummeled by [[laser]] fire, Willard ordered all power to be routed to the [[deflector shield]]s, but a crewmember stated that they were quickly losing them. In response, Willard ordered that the ship be abandoned, but the ship was quickly annihilated as Willard told his crew that [[May the Force be with you|may the Force be with them all]]. An enormous eruption engulfed the cruiser, and [[Darth]] Vader looked on from the [[Bridge (starship)|bridge]] of the ''Executor'', satisfied that the rebels now understood and that he felt their terror. ==Personality and traits== Willard, a [[human]] [[Gender|male]], had fair skin, [[Color|graying]] [[hair]], and dark [[eye]]s. ==Skills and abilities== As a general, Willard was able to command others. When his cruiser was attacked, he recognized that the shields required full power, but was not able to recognize earlier that the cruiser had to be evacuated. Willard had the ability to review flight simulator records, and make a determination on whether pilots were able to fly X-wing starfighters before recruiting them. ==Equipment== As an [[Alliance officer]], Willard was dressed in [[officer]] fatigues and wore the [[Rebel command insignia|command insignia]] on the left side of his chest. The insignia had five pips: four red pips on each corner and a blue pip at the center. While in command of Beta Group, Willard utilized a cruiser as his flagship. ==Behind the scenes== Willard first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], where he was physically portrayed by [[Wikipedia:Irish|Irish]] actor [[Eddie Byrne]] and voiced by [[Michael Bell]].[[Homing Beacon (newsletter)|''Homing Beacon'']] #260 Willard's first name was first mentioned in [[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]], which was released in [[2017]] and authored by [[Ryder Windham]] and edited by [[Emil Fortune]].[[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]] [[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'']] lead writer [[Kieron Gillen]] was the one who came up with killing off Willard, as he thought about how the heroes rose through the ranks so quickly.{{Twitter|kierongillen|status/1083315031297703937|[[Kieron Gillen]]|quote=I did. I came from me thinking "how did our heroes climb the ranks so quickly?" and the answer being "a huge disaster which created a lot of places in CoC."|archivefile=File:KieronGillen-Death-January-10-2019.png}} ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (LINE Webtoon)|''Star Wars'' LINE Webtoon]] *{{Film|IV}} {{1st}} *''[[A New Hope: The Princess, the Scoundrel, and the Farm Boy]]'' {{Ab|A New Hope: The Princess, the Scoundrel, and the Farm Boy (audiobook)}} *[[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]] {{1stID|Vanden}} *[[Star Wars (2015) 45|''Star Wars'' (2015) 45]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 50|''Star Wars'' (2015) 50]] ===Non-canon appearances=== *''[[Disney Magic Kingdoms]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{HelmetCollectionCite|26|Weapons & Uniforms}} *''[[Star Wars: Geektionary: The Galaxy from A - Z]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]'' *{{InsiderCite|196|The General's Voice: Michael Bell}} *''[[Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy]]'' *{{EncyclopediaCite|The Rebel Alliance}} *''[[Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide]]'' {{1stID|Huyck}} *''[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]'' *''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes I–VI, 1977–2005]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Vanden Huyck Willard |ja=ヴァンデン・ハイク・ウィラード |ka=ვანდენ ჰაიკ უილარდი }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Willard, Vanden}} [[Category:Alderaanians]] [[Category:Alliance admirals and generals]] [[Category:Alliance High Command personnel]] [[Category:Alliance sector commanders]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Massassi Group personnel]]
